title: pilosella bauhini subsp. thaumasioides
intro: Pilosella bauhini subsp. thaumasioides, also known as a subspecies of the Pilosella bauhini plant, is a rare and lesser-known species of flowering plant in the dandelion family, native to parts of Europe and Asia. This plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which contribute to its potential health benefits. The health benefits of Pilosella bauhini subsp. thaumasioides include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and support the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically used in the form of infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be made by drying the leaves and flowers and then steeping them in hot water or a solvent such as ethanol.
